content.text:
A TablePay Restaurant Ordering & Community system with main features including offline payment capture and reconciliation, secure refund handling, rich dish and option modeling, high-concurrency ordering performance, and community engagement with governance. Customers use an English UI rendered server-side and progressively enhanced with HTMX so menu browsing, option selection, and cart updates feel instant without full page reloads; each dish can be filtered by category, tags, availability windows (for example, “Lunch 11:00 AM–2:30 PM”), and sold-out status, with clear required-option prompts for spice level, soup base, or portion size before checkout. Store Managers publish or unpublish items, set sort order, manage images, and configure pricing strategies such as add-ons and size upcharges; image uploads validate file type and size (JPEG/PNG only, max 2 MB) and show immediate preview and error feedback. Finance Admins work from a payments workspace that supports “gateway-like” flows in a fully offline environment: the system can run against a locally hosted WeChat Pay (JSAPI)-compatible simulator for testing and training, and it can also ingest real-world payment statements exported from terminals as CSV for reconciliation, showing variances, exception logs, and an operator workflow to resolve mismatches. Community features allow logged-in users to like, favorite, and comment on dishes or posts, report abusive content, and block other users; Moderators review a queue of flagged items with reason codes and outcomes, while the UI throttles rapid actions and clearly communicates when a user hits a posting cooldown.

The backend uses Flask to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by HTMX requests, enforcing server-side validation on every parameter and returning consistent, human-readable error states; SQLite persists users, roles, dishes, options, inventory state, payments, refunds, reconciliation runs, and moderation events entirely on local disk. Authentication is local username and password only, with bcrypt password hashing, a minimum of 12 characters, lockout after 10 failed attempts in 15 minutes, and CSRF protection on cookie-based sessions; anti-replay uses per-request nonces with a 5-minute expiration for sensitive operations such as refunds and role changes. Payment callbacks are supported without internet dependency by accepting signed “callback packages” generated by the local gateway simulator or imported from files; signature verification uses pre-shared rotating keys stored encrypted at rest, and idempotency prevents duplicate callbacks using a 24-hour uniqueness window per transaction reference. Refunds support partial and multiple refunds up to the captured amount, enforce original-route refund rules, and trigger step-up confirmation requiring re-entry of the manager password when the refund exceeds $50.00 or when risk checks detect anomalies such as more than 3 refunds from the same device in 30 minutes. High availability is achieved through async job processing backed by a SQLite job table for tasks like reconciliation and bulk menu updates, plus caching with short TTLs (for example, 60 seconds for menu reads), rate limiting (for example, 60 requests per minute per user), circuit breaking for overloaded endpoints, structured exception logging, nightly encrypted backups retained for 14 days, and a tested restore procedure that can rebuild the system on a new machine with no network access.

so make sure this problems are fixed and the app is build according to the the above prompt give na fits perfectly and i'll test the app using this prompt "You are the reviewer responsible for Delivery Acceptance and Project Architecture Audit.

Work only within the current working directory.

This is a static-only audit.
Do not start the project.
Do not run Docker.
Do not run tests automatically.
Do not modify code.
Do not infer runtime success from documentation alone.

Your job is to find as many material issues as possible, especially Blocker / High severity issues, while keeping all conclusions evidence-based and traceable.

[Business / Task Prompt]
{prompt}

[Acceptance / Scoring Criteria (the only authority)]
{
1. Hard Gates

1.1 Documentation and static verifiability
- Whether clear startup / run / test / configuration instructions are provided
- Whether the documented entry points, configuration, and project structure appear statically consistent
- Whether the delivery provides enough static evidence for a human reviewer to attempt verification without first rewriting core code

1.2 Whether the delivered project materially deviates from the Prompt
- Whether the implementation is centered on the business goal or usage scenario described in the Prompt
- Whether there are major parts of the implementation that are only loosely related, or unrelated, to the Prompt
- Whether the project replaces, weakens, or ignores the core problem definition in the Prompt without justification

2. Delivery Completeness

2.1 Whether the delivered project fully covers the core requirements explicitly stated in the Prompt
- Whether all explicitly stated core functional requirements in the Prompt are implemented

2.2 Whether the delivered project represents a basic end-to-end deliverable from 0 to 1, rather than a partial feature, illustrative implementation, or code fragment
- Whether mock / hardcoded behavior is used in place of real logic without explanation
- Whether the project includes a complete project structure rather than scattered code or a single-file example
- Whether basic project documentation is provided, such as a README or equivalent

3. Engineering and Architecture Quality

3.1 Whether the project adopts a reasonable engineering structure and module decomposition for the scale of the problem
- Whether the project structure is clear and module responsibilities are reasonably defined
- Whether the project contains redundant or unnecessary files
- Whether the implementation is excessively piled into a single file

3.2 Whether the project shows basic maintainability and extensibility, rather than being a temporary or stacked implementation
- Whether there are obvious signs of chaotic structure or tight coupling
- Whether the core logic leaves room for extension rather than being completely hard-coded

4. Engineering Details and Professionalism

4.1 Whether the engineering details and overall shape reflect professional software practice, including but not limited to error handling, logging, validation, and API design
- Whether error handling is basically reliable and user-friendly
- Whether logs support troubleshooting rather than being random print statements or completely absent
- Whether necessary validation is present for key inputs and boundary conditions

4.2 Whether the project is organized like a real product or service, rather than remaining at the level of an example or demo
- Whether the overall deliverable resembles a real application instead of a teaching sample or demonstration-only project

5. Prompt Understanding and Requirement Fit

5.1 Whether the project accurately understands and responds to the business goal, usage scenario, and implicit constraints described in the Prompt, rather than merely implementing surface-level technical features
- Whether the core business objective in the Prompt is implemented correctly
- Whether there are obvious misunderstandings of the requirement semantics or deviations from the actual problem
- Whether key constraints in the Prompt are changed or ignored without explanation

6. Aesthetics (frontend-only / full-stack tasks only)

6.1 Whether the visual and interaction design fits the scenario and demonstrates reasonable visual quality
- Whether different functional areas of the page are visually distinguishable through background, spacing, separation, or hierarchy
- Whether the overall layout is reasonable, and whether alignment, spacing, and proportions are broadly consistent
- Whether UI elements, including text, images, and icons, render and display correctly
- Whether visual elements are consistent with the page theme and textual content, and whether there are obvious mismatches between images, illustrations, decorative elements, and the actual content
- Whether basic interaction feedback is provided, such as hover states, click states, or transitions, so users can understand the current interaction state
- Whether fonts, font sizes, colors, and icon styles are generally consistent, without obvious visual inconsistency or mixed design language
}

====================
Hard Rules (must follow)

1) Static-only audit boundary
- Perform static analysis only.
- Do not run the project, tests, Docker, or external services.
- Do not claim that a flow works at runtime unless this is directly proven by static evidence such as implementation completeness plus tests that clearly cover the flow.
- Any conclusion that depends on real runtime behavior, environment setup, network access, container orchestration, browser interaction, timing, or external integrations must be marked as:
  - Cannot Confirm Statistically
  - or Manual Verification Required

2) Prompt-to-code alignment is mandatory
- Prompt is the core constraint for the whole audit.
- You must extract the core business goal, main flows, explicit requirements, and important implicit constraints from the Prompt.
- You must compare those requirement points against the actual code, structure, interfaces, data model, tests, and documentation.
- Do not review the repository as a generic codebase only; always judge it against the Prompt.

3) Risk-first scan order is allowed and recommended
- You do not need to review in the same order as the six acceptance sections.
- You may scan in any order that improves speed and accuracy, but the final report must still be organized by the six major acceptance sections.
- Recommended scan priority:
  1. README / docs / config examples / manifests / env examples
  2. Entry points and route registration
  3. Authentication / session / token / middleware / permission guards
  4. Core business modules, services, data models, persistence layer
  5. Admin / internal / debug endpoints
  6. Test files and test configuration
  7. Frontend UI structure and visual consistency if applicable

4) Do not omit material findings, but avoid wasteful repetition
- The final report must cover every major acceptance section and every secondary requirement under it.
- If an item is not applicable, mark it as Not Applicable and explain the boundary briefly.
- If an item cannot be proven statically, mark it as Cannot Confirm Statistically and explain why.
- Merge repeated symptoms into root-cause findings where appropriate.
- Do not expand low-value duplicate issues across many files if they are caused by the same root cause.

5) Evidence must be traceable
- Every key conclusion must include precise evidence with file path + line number, such as `README.md:10` or `app/main.py:42`.
- Do not make unsupported judgments.
- Strong conclusions such as Pass / Fail / Blocker / High must not be based on vague impressions.
- Missing in the reviewed scope does not automatically mean missing in the repository; only conclude Missing when the reviewed evidence is sufficient to support that conclusion. Otherwise use Cannot Confirm Statistically.

6) Every judgment must be justified
- For every judgment such as Pass / Partial Pass / Fail / Not Applicable / Cannot Confirm Statistically, explain the reasoning briefly and concretely.
- The basis may come from:
  - alignment against the Prompt
  - alignment against the acceptance criteria
  - alignment against common engineering / architectural practice
  - code-level implementation evidence
  - static test evidence
  - documentation-to-code consistency evidence

7) Security review has priority
- Pay special attention to authentication, authorization, privilege boundaries, and data isolation.
- You must explicitly examine and assess, with evidence:
  - authentication entry points
  - route-level authorization
  - object-level authorization
  - function-level authorization
  - tenant / user data isolation
  - admin / internal / debug endpoint protection
- If a likely security risk is suggested but not fully provable statically, mark it as Suspected Risk or Cannot Confirm Statistically rather than overstating it.

8) Mock / stub / fake handling
- Mock / stub / fake behavior is not a defect by itself unless the Prompt or documentation explicitly requires real third-party integration or real production behavior.
- You must still explain:
  - how the mock behavior is implemented
  - under what conditions it is enabled
  - whether there is a risk of accidental production use
  - whether validation or safety checks can be bypassed because of it

9) Tests and logging are mandatory review dimensions
- You must statically assess unit tests, API / integration tests, and logging.
- Do not run them.
- Assess:
  - whether they exist
  - what they appear to cover
  - whether they cover core flows and important failure paths
  - whether logging categories are meaningful
  - whether there is a sensitive-data leakage risk in logs or responses

10) Static audit of test coverage is mandatory, but keep it risk-focused
- First extract the core business requirements and major risk points from the Prompt and implementation.
- Then map the most important requirement / risk points to the existing tests.
- Focus especially on:
  - core happy path
  - input validation failures
  - unauthenticated 401
  - unauthorized 403
  - not found 404
  - conflict / duplicate submission where relevant
  - object-level authorization
  - tenant / user isolation
  - pagination / sorting / filtering where relevant
  - empty data / extreme values / time fields / concurrency / repeated requests / rollback where relevant
  - sensitive log exposure
- Build coverage mapping for high-risk and core requirement areas first.
- You do not need to produce a bloated exhaustive matrix for every trivial requirement if the same root cause or same coverage gap already explains the risk.
- Still clearly state which important risks are sufficiently covered, insufficiently covered, missing, not applicable, or cannot confirm.

11) Severity rating
- Every issue must be severity-rated as:
  - Blocker
  - High
  - Medium
  - Low
- Prioritize reporting independent root-cause issues over repeated surface symptoms.
- For each issue include:
  - severity
  - conclusion
  - evidence
  - impact
  - minimum actionable fix
  - minimal verification path or manual verification suggestion if useful

12) No code modification
- This task is review only.
- Do not modify code to make the project appear to pass.
- If changes would be required, record them under Issues / Suggestions only.

====================
Output Requirements

Produce the final audit in a concise but complete report and write the consolidated report to `./.tmp/**.md`.

The final report must be organized by the six major acceptance sections in order, even if your scan order was different.

Use this structure:

1. Verdict
- Overall conclusion: Pass / Partial Pass / Fail / Cannot Confirm Statistically

2. Scope and Static Verification Boundary
- What was reviewed
- What was not reviewed
- What was intentionally not executed
- Which claims require manual verification

3. Repository / Requirement Mapping Summary
- Summarize the Prompt’s core business goal, core flows, and major constraints
- Summarize the main implementation areas you mapped against those requirements
- This section should be short and functional, not verbose

4. Section-by-section Review

For each major section and each secondary item under it, provide:
- Conclusion: Pass / Partial Pass / Fail / Not Applicable / Cannot Confirm Statistically
- Rationale: brief reasoning tied to Prompt and code
- Evidence: `file:line`
- Manual verification note only if static proof is insufficient and human follow-up is needed

5. Issues / Suggestions (Severity-Rated)

List all material issues found.
For each issue provide:
- Severity
- Title
- Conclusion
- Evidence (`file:line`)
- Impact
- Minimum actionable fix

Rules for issue listing:
- Report Blocker / High issues first
- Merge duplicate manifestations caused by the same root cause
- Do not inflate severity for style-only issues
- Do not treat lack of runtime execution as a defect
- Do not treat acceptable mocks as defects unless they violate the Prompt

6. Security Review Summary

Provide explicit conclusions, with evidence, for:
- authentication entry points
- route-level authorization
- object-level authorization
- function-level authorization
- tenant / user isolation
- admin / internal / debug protection

For each one, use:
- Pass / Partial Pass / Fail / Cannot Confirm Statistically
- plus brief evidence and reasoning

7. Tests and Logging Review

Provide separate conclusions for:
- Unit tests
- API / integration tests
- Logging categories / observability
- Sensitive-data leakage risk in logs / responses

8. Test Coverage Assessment (Static Audit)

This section is mandatory.

It must contain:

8.1 Test Overview
- Whether unit tests and API / integration tests exist
- Test framework(s)
- Test entry points
- Whether documentation provides test commands
- Evidence (`file:line`)

8.2 Coverage Mapping Table
For each core requirement or high-risk point reviewed, provide:
- Requirement / Risk Point
- Mapped Test Case(s) (`file:line`)
- Key Assertion / Fixture / Mock (`file:line`)
- Coverage Assessment: sufficient / basically covered / insufficient / missing / not applicable / cannot confirm
- Gap
- Minimum Test Addition

8.3 Security Coverage Audit
Provide coverage conclusions for:
- authentication
- route authorization
- object-level authorization
- tenant / data isolation
- admin / internal protection
For each, explain whether tests meaningfully cover the risk or whether severe defects could still remain undetected.

8.4 Final Coverage Judgment
The conclusion must be exactly one of:
- Pass
- Partial Pass
- Fail
- Cannot Confirm

Then explain the boundary clearly:
- which major risks are covered
- which uncovered risks mean the tests could still pass while severe defects remain

9. Final Notes
- Keep the report precise, evidence-based, and non-repetitive.
- Do not pad the report with generic advice.
- Do not overstate what static analysis can prove.

====================
Review Discipline

Before finalizing any strong conclusion, ask yourself:
- Is this directly supported by file:line evidence?
- Is this a static fact, or am I implying runtime behavior?
- Am I reporting a root cause, or only repeating symptoms?
- Have I judged this against the Prompt rather than generic preferences?
- If uncertain, should this be Cannot Confirm Statistically instead?

Your priority is:
1. Find real material defects
2. Keep conclusions evidence-based
3. Reduce hallucination
4. Preserve final completeness
5. Avoid unnecessary repetition

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me provide a comprehensive summary.

---

## TablePay Restaurant Ordering - Complete Codebase Structure

### 1. DIRECTORY TREE (Top-Level and Key Subdirectories)

```
TablePay-Restaurant-Ordering/
├── docs/                          # Root documentation
├── repo/                           # Main application repository
│   ├── README.md                   # Main project README
│   ├── docker-compose.yml
│   ├── Dockerfile.tests
│   ├── run_tests.sh
│   ├── backend/                    # Flask backend application
│   │   ├── app/                    # Main application package
│   │   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   │   ├── factory.py          # Flask app factory (route registration)
│   │   │   ├── config.py
│   │   │   ├── extensions.py
│   │   │   ├── cli.py
│   │   │   ├── logging.py
│   │   │   ├── wsgi.py
│   │   │   ├── controllers/        # Request handlers (9 controllers)
│   │   │   ├── routes/             # Blueprint route registration (10 blueprints)
│   │   │   ├── services/           # Business logic (15+ services)
│   │   │   ├── repositories/       # Data access layer (9 repositories)
│   │   │   ├── models/             # SQLAlchemy ORM models (11 model files)
│   │   │   ├── templates/          # Jinja2 HTML templates
│   │   │   │   ├── auth/
│   │   │   │   ├── home/
│   │   │   │   ├── menu/
│   │   │   │   ├── cart/
│   │   │   │   ├── community/
│   │   │   │   ├── finance/
│   │   │   │   ├── manager/
│   │   │   │   ├── moderation/
│   │   │   │   ├── orders/
│   │   │   │   ├── reconciliation/
│   │   │   │   ├── admin/
│   │   │   │   ├── errors/
│   │   │   │   ├── partials/
│   │   │   │   └── base.html
│   │   │   └── static/
│   │   │       ├── css/
│   │   │       └── js/
│   │   ├── migrations/             # Alembic database migrations (9 versions)
│   │   ├── API_tests/              # Integration tests (10 API test files)
│   │   ├── unit_tests/             # Unit tests (14 test files)
│   │   ├── scripts/                # Utility scripts
│   │   ├── conftest.py             # Pytest fixtures
│   │   ├── manage.py
│   │   ├── pyproject.toml
│   │   ├── requirements.txt
│   │   └── Dockerfile
│   └── frontend/                   # Frontend-facing tests (no SPA)
│       ├── API_tests/              # SSR route tests
│       ├── unit_tests/             # Template component tests
│       ├── e2e/                    # End-to-end browser tests
│       └── conftest.py
└── sessions/                       # Session data (runtime)
```

### 2. DOCUMENTATION FOLDERS

**Docs location:** `./docs/`

Files in docs/:
- **api-spec.md** - Complete API specification with error contracts, pagination format, and all endpoint definitions
- **design.md** - Architecture overview, security, data model, testing strategy
- **disaster-recovery-runbook.md** - Backup/restore procedures
- **questions.md** - Q&A documentation

**Repo README:** `/repo/README.md` - Docker setup, test running, default credentials, disaster recovery reference

---

### 3. MAIN APP ENTRY POINT AND ROUTE REGISTRATION

**Entry Point:** `./repo/backend/app/factory.py` (`create_app()` function)

**Route Registration in factory.py (lines 44-53):**

```python
app.register_blueprint(auth_bp)
app.register_blueprint(catalog_bp)
app.register_blueprint(orders_bp)
app.register_blueprint(payments_bp)
app.register_blueprint(reconciliation_bp)
app.register_blueprint(refunds_bp)
app.register_blueprint(community_bp)
app.register_blueprint(moderation_bp)
app.register_blueprint(ops_bp)
app.register_blueprint(pages_bp)
```

**Blueprints defined in:** `./repo/backend/app/routes/__init__.py`

**All blueprints imported from:**
- `./repo/backend/app/routes/auth.py`
- `./repo/backend/app/routes/catalog.py`
- `./repo/backend/app/routes/orders.py`
- `./repo/backend/app/routes/payments.py`
- `./repo/backend/app/routes/reconciliation.py`
- `./repo/backend/app/routes/refunds.py`
- `./repo/backend/app/routes/community.py`
- `./repo/backend/app/routes/moderation.py`
- `./repo/backend/app/routes/ops.py`
- `./repo/backend/app/routes/pages.py`

---

### 4. COMMUNITY-RELATED ENDPOINTS/ROUTES

**File:** `./repo/backend/app/routes/community.py`

**Controller:** `./repo/backend/app/controllers/community_controller.py`

**Endpoints:**

| Method | Route | Function | Description |
|--------|-------|----------|-------------|
| GET | `/community` | `community_page()` | Page: view all posts |
| POST | `/api/community/likes/toggle` | `toggle_like()` | Toggle like on post/dish |
| POST | `/api/community/favorites/toggle` | `toggle_favorite()` | Toggle favorite on post/dish |
| POST | `/api/community/comments` | `create_comment()` | Create comment on post |
| POST | `/api/community/reports` | `create_report()` | Report abuse/spam |
| POST | `/api/community/blocks` | `block_user()` | Block a user |
| DELETE | `/api/community/blocks/<blocked_user_id>` | `unblock_user()` | Unblock a user |

**Models:** `./repo/backend/app/models/community.py`
- Post
- Comment
- Like
- Favorite
- Report
- UserBlock
- CooldownEvent

**Database Tables:**
- `posts` - User-created posts with author, title, body
- `comments` - Comments on posts (target_type, target_id pattern)
- `likes` - Like records (unique constraint: user_id, target_type, target_id)
- `favorites` - Favorite records (same unique constraint)
- `reports` - Abuse reports with reason_code field
- `user_blocks` - User-to-user blocking (unique constraint: user_id, blocked_user_id)
- `cooldown_events` - Rate limiting for rapid actions

---

### 5. FINANCE/PAYMENT-RELATED ENDPOINTS/ROUTES

**Payment Routes:** `./repo/backend/app/routes/payments.py`
**Reconciliation Routes:** `./repo/backend/app/routes/reconciliation.py`
**Refund Routes:** `./repo/backend/app/routes/refunds.py`

#### Payment Endpoints

| Method | Route | Function | Description |
|--------|-------|----------|-------------|
| GET | `/finance/payments` | `finance_workspace()` | Page: payment management workspace |
| POST | `/api/payments/capture` | `capture_payment()` | Record offline payment capture |
| POST | `/api/payments/callbacks/import` | `import_callbacks()` | Import payment gateway callback |
| POST | `/api/payments/callbacks/verify` | `verify_callbacks()` | Verify callback signature |
| POST | `/api/payments/jsapi/simulate` | `simulate_jsapi_callback()` | Test simulator callback |
| GET | `/api/payments/<payment_id>` | `get_payment()` | Fetch payment details |

#### Reconciliation Endpoints

| Method | Route | Function | Description |
|--------|-------|----------|-------------|
| GET | `/finance/reconciliation` | `reconciliation_dashboard()` | Page: reconciliation dashboard |
| POST | `/api/finance/reconciliation/import` | `import_reconciliation_csv()` | Import CSV statement (sync or async) |
| POST | `/api/finance/reconciliation/import/async` | `enqueue_reconciliation_import()` | Queue async import |
| GET | `/api/finance/reconciliation/runs` | `list_runs()` | List reconciliation runs (paginated) |
| GET | `/api/finance/reconciliation/runs/<run_id>` | `get_run()` | Get run details with exceptions |
| POST | `/api/finance/reconciliation/exceptions/<exception_id>/resolve` | `resolve_exception()` | Resolve variance exception |

#### Refund Endpoints

| Method | Route | Function | Description |
|--------|-------|----------|-------------|
| GET | `/finance/refunds` | `refund_page()` | Page: refund management |
| POST | `/api/refunds` | `create_refund()` | Create refund (requires nonce) |
| GET | `/api/refunds/<refund_id>` | `get_refund()` | Get refund status/history |
| POST | `/api/refunds/<refund_id>/confirm-stepup` | `confirm_stepup()` | Manager password re-entry (requires nonce) |
| GET | `/api/refunds/risk-events` | `list_risk_events()` | List risk events (paginated) |

**Models:**
- `./repo/backend/app/models/payments.py` - PaymentTransaction, PaymentCallback, GatewaySigningKey, CallbackDedupKey
- `./repo/backend/app/models/reconciliation.py` - ReconciliationRun, ReconciliationRow, ReconciliationException, ReconciliationAction
- `./repo/backend/app/models/refunds.py` - Refund, RefundEvent, RefundRiskEvent, ManagerStepupChallenge

---

### 6. ALL TEST FILES

**Backend Unit Tests (14 files):**
```
./repo/backend/unit_tests/
├── test_auth_service.py
├── test_catalog_service.py
├── test_community_service.py
├── test_config.py
├── test_error_sanitization.py
├── test_moderation_service.py
├── test_ops_service.py
├── test_order_service.py
├── test_password_policy.py
├── test_payment_service.py
├── test_rbac.py
├── test_reconciliation_service.py
├── test_refund_service.py
└── test_time_utils.py
```

**Backend API/Integration Tests (10 files):**
```
./repo/backend/API_tests/
├── test_auth_api.py (155 lines)

---

### 8. PAGINATION PATTERNS

**Core Implementation:** `./repo/backend/app/controllers/pagination.py`

**Functions:**

1. **`parse_pagination_args(args, default_page_size=20, max_page_size=100)`**
   - Extracts `page` and `page_size` from query args
   - Returns dict with: `requested`, `page`, `page_size`
   - Validates positive integers
   - Caps `page_size` at `max_page_size`

2. **`paginate_collection(items: Sequence[T], pagination: dict)`**
   - In-memory pagination for collections
   - Calculates total_pages, has_next, has_prev
   - Returns: (page_items, pagination_meta)
   - Pagination meta includes: `page`, `page_size`, `total_items`, `total_pages`, `has_next`, `has_prev`

**API Response Format** (from api-spec.md):
```json
{
  "page": 1,
  "page_size": 20,
  "total_items": 42,
  "total_pages": 3,
  "has_next": true,
  "has_prev": false
}
```

- `catalog_controller.py` - List dishes
- `moderation_controller.py` - Moderation queue
- `ops_controller.py` - Jobs, rate limits, circuit breakers (3 endpoints)
- `reconciliation_controller.py` - Reconciliation runs
- `refund_controller.py` - Risk events

**Query Parameters:** `?page=1&page_size=20` (optional on all list endpoints)

---

### SUMMARY OF KEY FILES

**Architecture Layer Files:**
- **Factory:** `factory.py` (211 lines) - App creation, blueprint registration, error handlers, middleware
- **Routes:** 10 blueprint files in `routes/` directory
- **Controllers:** 10 controllers in `controllers/` directory
- **Services:** 15+ service classes handling business logic
- **Repositories:** 9 repository classes for data access
- **Models:** 11 model definition files

**Configuration & Infrastructure:**

- `cli.py` - CLI commands

- `wsgi.py` - WSGI entry point

**Security & Utilities:**
- `services/auth_service.py` - Session, CSRF, authentication
- `services/errors.py` - AppError and error sanitization
- `services/password_policy.py` - Password validation
- `services/payment_security.py` - Payment callback signing
- `services/rbac_service.py` - Role-based access control

**Database:**
- 9 migration version files (20260328_0001 through 0009)
- SQLite-based with Alembic migrations
- Models in: auth, catalog, ordering, payments, reconciliation, refunds, community, moderation, ops

---

This comprehensive codebase uses a clean **routes → controllers → services → repositories → models** architecture with Flask, SQLAlchemy, Jinja2 templates, and SQLite persistence.

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import json
4	from datetime import timedelta
5	
6	import structlog
7	
8	from app.extensions import db
9	from app.repositories.community_repository import CommunityRepository
10	from app.repositories.moderation_repository import ModerationRepository
11	from app.services.errors import AppError
12	from app.services.moderation_service import ModerationService
13	from app.services.time_utils import serialize_utc_datetime, utc_now_naive
14	
15	
16	logger = structlog.get_logger(__name__)
17	
18	
19	class CommunityService:
20	    def __init__(self, repository: CommunityRepository) -> None:
21	        self.repository = repository
22	
23	    def toggle_like(self, user_id: str, payload: dict):
24	        target_type = (payload.get("target_type") or "").strip()
25	        target_id = (payload.get("target_id") or "").strip()
26	        self._validate_target(target_type, target_id)
27	        self._enforce_throttle(user_id, "like_toggle", target_type, target_id)
28	        like = self.repository.get_like(user_id, target_type, target_id)
29	        active = False
30	        if like is None:
31	            self.repository.create_like(user_id=user_id, target_type=target_type, target_id=target_id)
32	            active = True
33	        else:
34	            self.repository.delete_like(like)
35	        db.session.commit()
36	        return {"active": active, "count": self.repository.count_likes(target_type, target_id)}
37	
38	    def toggle_favorite(self, user_id: str, payload: dict):
39	        target_type = (payload.get("target_type") or "").strip()
40	        target_id = (payload.get("target_id") or "").strip()
41	        self._validate_target(target_type, target_id)
42	        self._enforce_throttle(user_id, "favorite_toggle", target_type, target_id)
43	        favorite = self.repository.get_favorite(user_id, target_type, target_id)
44	        active = False
45	        if favorite is None:
46	            self.repository.create_favorite(user_id=user_id, target_type=target_type, target_id=target_id)
47	            active = True
48	        else:
49	            self.repository.delete_favorite(favorite)
50	        db.session.commit()
51	        return {"active": active, "count": self.repository.count_favorites(target_type, target_id)}
52	
53	    def create_comment(self, user, payload: dict):
54	        target_type = (payload.get("target_type") or "").strip()
55	        target_id = (payload.get("target_id") or "").strip()
56	        body = (payload.get("body") or "").strip()
57	        self._validate_target(target_type, target_id)
58	        if len(body) < 3:
59	            raise AppError("validation_error", "Comment must be at least 3 characters.", 400)
60	        self._enforce_throttle(user.id, "comment", target_type, target_id)
61	        self._enforce_cooldown(user.id, "comment", target_type, target_id, seconds=30)
62	        self._enforce_block_rules(user.id, target_type, target_id)
63	        comment = self.repository.create_comment(author_user_id=user.id, target_type=target_type, target_id=target_id, body=body)
64	        db.session.commit()
65	        logger.info("community.comment_created", comment_id=comment.id, user_id=user.id)
66	        return comment
67	
68	    def create_report(self, user, payload: dict):
69	        target_type = (payload.get("target_type") or "").strip()
70	        target_id = (payload.get("target_id") or "").strip()
71	        reason_code = (payload.get("reason_code") or "").strip()
72	        details = (payload.get("details") or "").strip()
73	        self._validate_target(target_type, target_id)
74	        if reason_code not in {"abuse", "spam", "harassment", "other"}:
75	            raise AppError("validation_error", "Invalid report reason code.", 400)
76	        self._enforce_throttle(user.id, "report", target_type, target_id)
77	        report = self.repository.create_report(
78	            reporter_user_id=user.id,
79	            target_type=target_type,
80	            target_id=target_id,
81	            reason_code=reason_code,
82	            details=details,
83	            status="open",
84	        )
85	        db.session.commit()
86	        ModerationService(ModerationRepository()).ensure_queue_item_for_report(report)
87	        return report
88	
89	    def block_user(self, user_id: str, blocked_user_id: str):
90	        if user_id == blocked_user_id:
91	            raise AppError("validation_error", "You cannot block yourself.", 400)
92	        existing = self.repository.get_block(user_id, blocked_user_id)
93	        if existing is None:
94	            self.repository.create_block(user_id=user_id, blocked_user_id=blocked_user_id)
95	            db.session.commit()
96	        return {"blocked_user_id": blocked_user_id}
97	
98	    def unblock_user(self, user_id: str, blocked_user_id: str):
99	        existing = self.repository.get_block(user_id, blocked_user_id)
100	        if existing is None:
101	            raise AppError("not_found", "Block record not found.", 404)
102	        self.repository.delete_block(existing)
103	        db.session.commit()
104	        return {"blocked_user_id": blocked_user_id}
105	
106	    def list_posts(self):
107	        return self.repository.list_posts()
108	
109	    def list_comments(self, target_type: str, target_id: str):
110	        return self.repository.list_comments(target_type, target_id)
111	
112	    def _validate_target(self, target_type: str, target_id: str) -> None:
113	        if target_type not in {"dish", "post"} or not target_id:
114	            raise AppError("validation_error", "Invalid target.", 400)
115	        if target_type == "post":
116	            if self.repository.get_post(target_id) is None:
117	                raise AppError("not_found", "Post not found.", 404)
118	            return
119	        if self.repository.get_dish(target_id) is None:
120	            raise AppError("not_found", "Dish not found.", 404)
121	
122	    def _enforce_throttle(self, user_id: str, action_type: str, target_type: str, target_id: str) -> None:
123	        now = utc_now_naive()
124	        recent = self.repository.recent_action_count(user_id, action_type, now - timedelta(minutes=1))
125	        if recent >= 5:
126	            raise AppError(
127	                "throttled",
128	                "Too many rapid community actions. Try again shortly.",
129	                429,
130	                {"retry_after_seconds": 60},
131	            )
132	        self.repository.create_cooldown_event(
133	            user_id=user_id,
134	            action_type=action_type,
135	            target_type=target_type,
136	            target_id=target_id,
137	            expires_at=now + timedelta(seconds=60),
138	            details_json=json.dumps({"kind": "throttle_window"}),
139	        )
140	
141	    def _enforce_cooldown(self, user_id: str, action_type: str, target_type: str, target_id: str, seconds: int) -> None:
142	        now = utc_now_naive()
143	        cooldown = self.repository.active_cooldown(user_id, f"{action_type}_cooldown", target_type, target_id, now)
144	        if cooldown is not None:
145	            raise AppError(
146	                "cooldown_active",
147	                "You need to wait before posting again.",
148	                429,
149	                {"expires_at": serialize_utc_datetime(cooldown.expires_at)},
150	            )
151	        self.repository.create_cooldown_event(
152	            user_id=user_id,
153	            action_type=f"{action_type}_cooldown",
154	            target_type=target_type,
155	            target_id=target_id,
156	            expires_at=now + timedelta(seconds=seconds),
157	            details_json=json.dumps({"seconds": seconds}),
158	        )
159	
160	    def _enforce_block_rules(self, user_id: str, target_type: str, target_id: str) -> None:
161	        if target_type != "post":
162	            return
163	        post = self.repository.get_post(target_id)
164	        if post and self.repository.block_exists_between(user_id, post.author_user_id):
165	            raise AppError("blocked_interaction", "You cannot interact with this user.", 403)

121	    )

130	def _load_callback_payload():
131	    payload = request.get_json(silent=True)
132	    if payload is not None:
133	        return payload
134	    if "package_file" in request.files:
135	        try:
136	            return json.loads(request.files["package_file"].read().decode("utf-8"))
137	        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
138	            raise AppError("validation_error", "package_file must contain valid JSON.", 400, {"error": str(exc)}) from exc
139	    raw_package = request.form.get("package_json", "{}")
140	    try:
141	        return json.loads(raw_package)
142	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
143	        raise AppError("validation_error", "package_json must contain valid JSON.", 400, {"error": str(exc)}) from exc

168	def resolve_exception(exception_id: str):
169	    _require_authenticated_user()
170	    payload = request.get_json(silent=True) or request.form
171	    exception = _service().resolve_exception(
172	        exception_id=exception_id,
173	        action_type=(payload.get("action_type") or "resolve").strip(),
174	        reason=(payload.get("reason") or "").strip(),
175	        operator_user_id=g.current_user.id,
176	        current_roles=g.current_roles,
177	    )
178	    run = _service().get_run(exception.run_id, g.current_roles)
179	    if request.headers.get("HX-Request") == "true":
180	        return attach_feedback(render_template("reconciliation/run_detail.html", run=run), "Exception resolved.")
181	    return jsonify({"code": "ok", "message": "Reconciliation exception updated.", "data": _serialize_exception(exception)})
182	
183	
184	def _parse_import_payload():
185	    json_payload = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
186	    if "statement_file" in request.files:
187	        csv_text = request.files["statement_file"].read().decode("utf-8")
188	        filename = request.files["statement_file"].filename or ""
189	    elif json_payload:
190	        csv_text = json_payload.get("statement_csv", "")
191	        filename = json_payload.get("filename", "")
192	    else:
193	        csv_text = request.form.get("statement_csv", "")
194	        filename = request.form.get("filename", "")
195	
196	    source_name = request.form.get("source_name") or json_payload.get("source_name", "terminal_csv")
197	    async_value = request.form.get("async") if request.form else None
198	    if async_value is None and isinstance(json_payload, dict):
199	        async_value = json_payload.get("async")
200	    async_requested = str(async_value).strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
201	    return csv_text, filename, source_name, async_requested
202

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from datetime import datetime
4	
5	from sqlalchemy import and_, func, or_, select
6	
7	from app.extensions import db
8	from app.models import Comment, CooldownEvent, Dish, Favorite, Like, Post, Report, UserBlock
9	
10	
11	class CommunityRepository:
12	    def get_dish(self, dish_id: str) -> Dish | None:
13	        stmt = select(Dish).where(Dish.id == dish_id)
14	        return db.session.scalar(stmt)
15	
16	    def get_post(self, post_id: str) -> Post | None:
17	        stmt = select(Post).where(Post.id == post_id)
18	        return db.session.scalar(stmt)
19	
20	    def get_comment(self, comment_id: str) -> Comment | None:
21	        stmt = select(Comment).where(Comment.id == comment_id)
22	        return db.session.scalar(stmt)
23	
24	    def create_post(self, **kwargs) -> Post:
25	        post = Post(**kwargs)
26	        db.session.add(post)
27	        db.session.flush()
28	        return post
29	
30	    def list_posts(self) -> list[Post]:
31	        stmt = select(Post).order_by(Post.created_at.desc())
32	        return list(db.session.scalars(stmt))
33	
34	    def list_comments(self, target_type: str, target_id: str) -> list[Comment]:
35	        stmt = select(Comment).where(Comment.target_type == target_type, Comment.target_id == target_id).order_by(Comment.created_at.asc())
36	        return list(db.session.scalars(stmt))
37	
38	    def create_comment(self, **kwargs) -> Comment:
39	        comment = Comment(**kwargs)
40	        db.session.add(comment)
41	        db.session.flush()
42	        return comment
43	
44	    def get_like(self, user_id: str, target_type: str, target_id: str) -> Like | None:
45	        stmt = select(Like).where(Like.user_id == user_id, Like.target_type == target_type, Like.target_id == target_id)
46	        return db.session.scalar(stmt)
47	
48	    def create_like(self, **kwargs) -> Like:
49	        like = Like(**kwargs)
50	        db.session.add(like)
51	        db.session.flush()
52	        return like
53	
54	    def delete_like(self, like: Like) -> None:
55	        db.session.delete(like)
56	        db.session.flush()
57	
58	    def count_likes(self, target_type: str, target_id: str) -> int:
59	        stmt = select(func.count()).select_from(Like).where(Like.target_type == target_type, Like.target_id == target_id)
60	        return db.session.scalar(stmt) or 0
61	
62	    def get_favorite(self, user_id: str, target_type: str, target_id: str) -> Favorite | None:
63	        stmt = select(Favorite).where(Favorite.user_id == user_id, Favorite.target_type == target_type, Favorite.target_id == target_id)
64	        return db.session.scalar(stmt)
65	
66	    def create_favorite(self, **kwargs) -> Favorite:
67	        favorite = Favorite(**kwargs)
68	        db.session.add(favorite)
69	        db.session.flush()
70	        return favorite
71	
72	    def delete_favorite(self, favorite: Favorite) -> None:
73	        db.session.delete(favorite)
74	        db.session.flush()
75	
76	    def count_favorites(self, target_type: str, target_id: str) -> int:
77	        stmt = select(func.count()).select_from(Favorite).where(Favorite.target_type == target_type, Favorite.target_id == target_id)
78	        return db.session.scalar(stmt) or 0
79	
80	    def create_report(self, **kwargs) -> Report:
81	        report = Report(**kwargs)
82	        db.session.add(report)
83	        db.session.flush()
84	        return report
85	
86	    def get_block(self, user_id: str, blocked_user_id: str) -> UserBlock | None:
87	        stmt = select(UserBlock).where(UserBlock.user_id == user_id, UserBlock.blocked_user_id == blocked_user_id)
88	        return db.session.scalar(stmt)
89	
90	    def create_block(self, **kwargs) -> UserBlock:
91	        block = UserBlock(**kwargs)
92	        db.session.add(block)
93	        db.session.flush()
94	        return block
95	
96	    def delete_block(self, block: UserBlock) -> None:
97	        db.session.delete(block)
98	        db.session.flush()
99	
100	    def block_exists_between(self, user_a: str, user_b: str) -> bool:
101	        stmt = select(UserBlock).where(
102	            or_(
103	                and_(UserBlock.user_id == user_a, UserBlock.blocked_user_id == user_b),
104	                and_(UserBlock.user_id == user_b, UserBlock.blocked_user_id == user_a),
105	            )
106	        )
107	        return db.session.scalar(stmt) is not None

19	REQUIRED_COLUMNS = {"transaction_reference", "amount", "currency", "status"}

22	class ReconciliationService:
23	    def __init__(self, repository: ReconciliationRepository) -> None:
24	        self.repository = repository
25	        self.rbac = RBACService()
26	
27	    def import_csv(
28	        self,
29	        csv_text: str,
30	        source_name: str,
31	        imported_filename: str,
32	        operator_user_id: str,
33	        current_roles: list[str],
34	    ):
35	        self.rbac.require_roles(current_roles, ["Finance Admin"])
36	        reader = csv.DictReader(StringIO(csv_text))
37	        if reader.fieldnames is None:
38	            raise AppError("validation_error", "CSV header row is required.", 400)
39	        missing_columns = REQUIRED_COLUMNS.difference(set(reader.fieldnames))
40	        if missing_columns:
41	            raise AppError(
42	                "validation_error",
43	                "CSV is missing required columns.",
44	                400,
45	                {"missing_columns": sorted(missing_columns)},
46	            )
47	
48	        run = self.repository.create_run(
49	            source_name=source_name or "terminal_csv",
50	            imported_by_user_id=operator_user_id,
51	            imported_filename=imported_filename or "",
52	            status="completed",
53	        )
54	
55	        seen_references: set[str] = set()
56	        matched_rows = 0
57	        exception_count = 0
58	
59	        for row_number, row in enumerate(reader, start=2):
60	            reference = (row.get("transaction_reference") or "").strip()
61	            if not reference:
62	                raise AppError("validation_error", "transaction_reference cannot be blank.", 400, {"row_number": row_number})
63	            amount = parse_price(row.get("amount"), "amount")
64	            status = (row.get("status") or "").strip().lower()
65	            currency = (row.get("currency") or "USD").strip().upper()
66	
67	            payments = self.repository.find_payments_by_reference(reference)
68	            match_status = "matched"
69	            exception_type = None
70	            details = {"terminal_row": row}
71	
72	            if reference in seen_references:
73	                match_status = "exception"
74	                exception_type = "duplicate_reference"
75	                details["reason"] = "Statement contains the same transaction reference more than once."
76	            elif not payments:
77	                match_status = "exception"
78	                exception_type = "missing_local_transaction"
79	                details["reason"] = "No local payment transaction matched the statement reference."
80	            elif len(payments) > 1:
81	                match_status = "exception"
82	                exception_type = "duplicate_reference"
83	                details["reason"] = "Multiple local payment transactions share the reference."
84	            else:
85	                payment = payments[0]
86	                details["payment_status"] = payment.status
87	                details["payment_amount"] = f"{payment.capture_amount:.2f}"
88	                if payment.capture_amount != amount:
89	                    match_status = "exception"
90	                    exception_type = "amount_mismatch"
91	                    details["reason"] = "Terminal amount does not match local capture amount."
92	                elif payment.status.lower() != status:
93	                    match_status = "exception"
94	                    exception_type = "status_mismatch"
95	                    details["reason"] = "Terminal status does not match local transaction status."
96	
97	            matched_payment_id = payments[0].id if len(payments) == 1 else None
98	            rec_row = self.repository.add_row(
99	                run_id=run.id,
100	                row_number=row_number,
101	                transaction_reference=reference,
102	                terminal_status=status,
103	                terminal_amount=amount,
104	                terminal_currency=currency,
105	                matched_payment_id=matched_payment_id,
106	                match_status=match_status,
107	                raw_row_json=json.dumps(row),
108	            )
109	
110	            if exception_type is not None:
111	                self.repository.add_exception(
112	                    run_id=run.id,
113	                    row_id=rec_row.id,
114	                    transaction_reference=reference,
115	                    exception_type=exception_type,
116	                    details_json=json.dumps(details),
117	                    status="open",
118	                )
119	                exception_count += 1
120	            else:
121	                matched_rows += 1
122	
123	            seen_references.add(reference)
124	
125	        run.total_rows = max(0, row_number - 1) if "row_number" in locals() else 0
126	        run.matched_rows = matched_rows
127	        run.exception_count = exception_count
128	        run.status = "completed_with_exceptions" if exception_count else "completed"
129	        db.session.add(run)
130	        db.session.commit()
131	        logger.info(
132	            "reconciliation.run_imported",
133	            run_id=run.id,
134	            total_rows=run.total_rows,
135	            matched_rows=matched_rows,
136	            exception_count=exception_count,
137	        )
138	        return run

140	    def enqueue_import_csv(
141	        self,
142	        csv_text: str,

144	        imported_filename: str,
145	        operator_user_id: str,

169	        return self.repository.list_runs()

171	    def get_run(self, run_id: str, current_roles: list[str]):
172	        self.rbac.require_roles(current_roles, ["Finance Admin"])

178	    def resolve_exception(
179	        self,
180	        exception_id: str,
181	        action_type: str,
182	        reason: str,
183	        operator_user_id: str,
184	        current_roles: list[str],
185	    ):
186	        self.rbac.require_roles(current_roles, ["Finance Admin"])
187	        exception = self.repository.get_exception(exception_id)
188	        if exception is None:
189	            raise AppError("not_found", "Reconciliation exception not found.", 404)
190	        if exception.status == "resolved":
191	            raise AppError("validation_error", "Exception is already resolved.", 400)
192	
193	        from_status = exception.status
194	        to_status = "resolved" if action_type == "resolve" else "open"
195	        exception.status = to_status
196	        exception.resolved_by_user_id = operator_user_id if to_status == "resolved" else None
197	        exception.resolved_at = utc_now_naive() if to_status == "resolved" else None
198	        exception.resolution_reason = reason.strip()
199	        db.session.add(exception)
200	        self.repository.add_action(
201	            exception_id=exception.id,
202	            operator_user_id=operator_user_id,
203	            action_type=action_type,
204	            from_status=from_status,
205	            to_status=to_status,
206	            reason=reason.strip(),
207	        )
208	
209	        run = self.repository.get_run(exception.run_id)
210	        open_exceptions = [item for item in run.exceptions if item.id != exception.id and item.status != "resolved"]
211	        if to_status == "resolved":
212	            run.exception_count = len(open_exceptions)
213	        run.status = "completed" if not open_exceptions and to_status == "resolved" else "completed_with_exceptions"
214	        db.session.add(run)
215	        db.session.commit()
216	        logger.info("reconciliation.exception_resolved", exception_id=exception.id, action_type=action_type, to_status=to_status)
217	        return exception

23	
24	class RefundService:
25	    def __init__(self, repository: RefundRepository) -> None:
26	        self.repository = repository
27	        self.rbac = RBACService()
28	        self.auth_service = AuthService(AuthRepository())
29	
30	    def create_refund(
31	        self,
32	        payload: dict,
33	        current_user,
34	        current_roles: list[str],
35	        current_session_id: str | None,
36	        nonce_value: str | None,
37	        device_id: str,
38	    ):
39	        self.rbac.require_roles(current_roles, ["Finance Admin"])
40	        self.auth_service.consume_nonce(current_session_id, "refund:create", nonce_value)
41	
42	        payment = self.repository.get_payment_by_reference((payload.get("transaction_reference") or "").strip())
43	        if payment is None:
44	            raise AppError("not_found", "Payment transaction not found.", 404)
45	        if payment.status != "success":
46	            raise AppError("validation_error", "Only successful captures can be refunded.", 400)
47	
48	        requested_amount = parse_price(payload.get("refund_amount"), "refund_amount")
49	        requested_route = (payload.get("route") or "").strip()
50	        if requested_route != payment.channel:
51	            raise AppError("original_route_required", "Refund must use the original capture route.", 400)
52	
53	        existing_refunds = self.repository.list_refunds_for_payment(payment.id)
54	        refunded_total = sum(
55	            refund.requested_amount
56	            for refund in existing_refunds
57	            if refund.status in {"approved", "pending_stepup", "held"}
58	        )
59	        if refunded_total + requested_amount > payment.capture_amount:
60	            raise AppError(
61	                "refund_cap_exceeded",
62	                "Refund total exceeds captured amount.",
63	                400,
64	                {"captured_amount": f"{payment.capture_amount:.2f}", "already_refunded": f"{refunded_total:.2f}"},
65	            )
66	
67	        risk_flags = []
68	        recent_device_refunds = self.repository.list_recent_device_refunds(device_id, utc_now_naive() - timedelta(minutes=30))
69	        if len(recent_device_refunds) >= 3:
70	            risk_flags.append({"risk_code": "device_refund_burst", "severity": "high", "action_taken": "hold"})
71	
72	        if requested_amount > Decimal("50.00"):
73	            risk_flags.append({"risk_code": "amount_stepup_threshold", "severity": "medium", "action_taken": "stepup"})
74	
75	        refund_status = "approved"
76	        hold_reason = ""
77	        stepup_required = "false"
78	        if risk_flags:
79	            refund_status = "pending_stepup"
80	            hold_reason = "; ".join(flag["risk_code"] for flag in risk_flags)
81	            stepup_required = "true"
82	
83	        refund = self.repository.create_refund(
84	            payment_transaction_id=payment.id,
85	            order_id=payment.order_id,
86	            transaction_reference=payment.transaction_reference,
87	            refund_reference=f"refund-{uuid4().hex[:12]}",
88	            original_route=requested_route,
89	            requested_amount=requested_amount,
90	            status=refund_status,
91	            requested_by_user_id=current_user.id,
92	            device_id=device_id,
93	            hold_reason=hold_reason,
94	            stepup_required=stepup_required,
95	            approved_at=utc_now_naive() if refund_status == "approved" else None,
96	        )
97	        self.repository.create_refund_event(
98	            refund_id=refund.id,
99	            event_type="created",
100	            from_status=None,
101	            to_status=refund_status,
102	            actor_user_id=current_user.id,
103	            details_json=json.dumps({"route": requested_route, "amount": f"{requested_amount:.2f}"}),
104	        )
105	
106	        for flag in risk_flags:
107	            self.repository.create_risk_event(
108	                refund_id=refund.id,
109	                payment_transaction_id=payment.id,
110	                device_id=device_id,
111	                risk_code=flag["risk_code"],
112	                severity=flag["severity"],
113	                action_taken=flag["action_taken"],
114	                details_json=json.dumps({"refund_amount": f"{requested_amount:.2f}"}),
115	            )
116	
117	        if refund_status == "pending_stepup":
118	            self.repository.create_stepup_challenge(
119	                refund_id=refund.id,
120	                operator_user_id=current_user.id,
121	                status="pending",
122	                reason=hold_reason or "step-up confirmation required",
123	                expires_at=utc_now_naive() + timedelta(minutes=5),
124	                completed_at=None,
125	            )
126	
127	        db.session.commit()
128	        logger.info("refund.created", refund_id=refund.id, payment_id=payment.id, status=refund_status)
129	        return refund
130	
131	    def confirm_stepup(
132	        self,
133	        refund_id: str,
134	        password: str,
135	        current_user,
136	        current_roles: list[str],
137	        current_session_id: str | None,
138	        nonce_value: str | None,
139	    ):
140	        self.rbac.require_roles(current_roles, ["Store Manager"])
141	        self.auth_service.consume_nonce(current_session_id, "refund:approve", nonce_value)
142	        refund = self.repository.get_refund(refund_id)
143	        if refund is None:
144	            raise AppError("not_found", "Refund not found.", 404)
145	        challenge = self.repository.get_active_stepup(refund.id)
146	        if challenge is None or challenge.expires_at < utc_now_naive():
147	            raise AppError("stepup_missing", "Active step-up challenge not found.", 400)
148	        if refund.status != "pending_stepup":
149	            raise AppError("stepup_missing", "Refund is not awaiting manager approval.", 400)
150	        if refund.requested_by_user_id == current_user.id or challenge.operator_user_id == current_user.id:
151	            raise AppError("approval_conflict", "The requesting operator cannot approve the same high-risk refund.", 403)
152	
153	        user = AuthRepository().get_user_by_username(current_user.username)
154	        if not password:
155	            raise AppError("stepup_failed", "Manager password confirmation is required.", 403)
156	        from app.extensions import bcrypt
157	
158	        if not bcrypt.check_password_hash(user.password_hash, password):
159	            raise AppError("stepup_failed", "Manager password confirmation failed.", 403)
160	
161	        from_status = refund.status
162	        refund.status = "approved"
163	        refund.approved_at = utc_now_naive()
164	        challenge.status = "completed"
165	        challenge.completed_at = utc_now_naive()
166	        db.session.add(refund)
167	        db.session.add(challenge)
168	        self.repository.create_refund_event(
169	            refund_id=refund.id,
170	            event_type="manager_stepup_approved",
171	            from_status=from_status,
172	            to_status="approved",
173	            actor_user_id=current_user.id,
174	            details_json=json.dumps(
175	                {
176	                    "challenge_id": challenge.id,
177	                    "approved_by_user_id": current_user.id,
178	                    "requested_by_user_id": refund.requested_by_user_id,
179	                }
180	            ),
181	        )
182	        db.session.commit()
183	        logger.info("refund.stepup_confirmed", refund_id=refund.id, approved_by_user_id=current_user.id)
184	        return refund
185	
186	    def get_refund(self, refund_id: str, current_roles: list[str]):
187	        self.rbac.require_roles(current_roles, ["Finance Admin", "Store Manager"])
188	        refund = self.repository.get_refund(refund_id)
189	        if refund is None:
190	            raise AppError("not_found", "Refund not found.", 404)
191	        return refund
192	
193	    def list_risk_events(self, current_roles: list[str]):
194	        self.rbac.require_roles(current_roles, ["Finance Admin", "Store Manager"])
195	        return self.repository.list_risk_events()
196	
197	    def list_workspace(self, current_roles: list[str]):
198	        self.rbac.require_roles(current_roles, ["Finance Admin", "Store Manager"])
199	        return self.repository.list_refunds(), self.repository.list_risk_events()

202	def bulk_update_dishes():
203	    if g.current_user is None:
204	        raise AppError("authentication_required", "Authentication is required.", 401)
205	    if request.is_json:
206	        payload = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
207	        dish_ids = payload.get("dish_ids") or []
208	    else:
209	        payload = request.form
210	        dish_ids = payload.getlist("dish_ids")
211	        if not dish_ids:
212	            dish_ids = [item.strip() for item in payload.get("dish_ids", "").split(",") if item.strip()]
213	
214	    if isinstance(dish_ids, str):
215	        dish_ids = [item.strip() for item in dish_ids.split(",") if item.strip()]
216	    publish = _coerce_optional_bool(payload.get("publish"))
217	    archived = _coerce_optional_bool(payload.get("archived"))
218	
219	    job = CatalogService(CatalogRepository()).queue_bulk_update(
220	        dish_ids=dish_ids,
221	        publish=publish,
222	        archived=archived,
223	        operator_user_id=g.current_user.id,
224	        current_roles=g.current_roles,
225	    )
226	    return (
227	        jsonify(
228	            {
229	                "code": "accepted",
230	                "message": "Bulk menu update queued.",
231	                "data": {"job_id": job.id, "status": job.status},
232	            }
233	        ),
234	        202,
235	    )

283	
284	def _inflate_payload(payload: dict) -> dict:
285	    if "tags" in payload and isinstance(payload["tags"], str):
286	        payload["tags"] = [tag.strip() for tag in payload["tags"].split(",") if tag.strip()]
287	    if "availability_windows" in payload and isinstance(payload["availability_windows"], str):
288	        payload["availability_windows"] = _load_json_field(payload["availability_windows"], "availability_windows")
289	    if "options" in payload and isinstance(payload["options"], str):
290	        payload["options"] = _load_json_field(payload["options"], "options")
291	    return payload
292	
293	
294	def _coerce_optional_bool(value):
295	    if value is None or value == "":
296	        return None
297	    if isinstance(value, bool):
298	        return value
299	    normalized = str(value).strip().lower()
300	    if normalized in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}:
301	        return True
302	    if normalized in {"0", "false", "no", "off"}:
303	        return False
304	    raise AppError("validation_error", "Boolean fields must be true or false.", 400)
305	
306	
307	def _load_json_field(raw_value: str, field_name: str):
308	    try:
309	        return json.loads(raw_value or "[]")
310	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
311	        raise AppError("validation_error", f"{field_name} must contain valid JSON.", 400, {"error": str(exc)}) from exc

136	def _inflate_payload(payload):
137	    data = payload.to_dict(flat=True)
138	    if "selected_options" in data and isinstance(data["selected_options"], str):
139	        data["selected_options"] = json.loads(data["selected_options"] or "{}")
140	    else:
141	        selected = {}
142	        for key in payload:
143	            if key.startswith("option_"):
144	                selected[key.removeprefix("option_")] = payload.getlist(key)
145	        data["selected_options"] = selected
146	    return data

77	    manager_csrf = login(manager_client, "manager", "Manager#12345")

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import math
4	from collections.abc import Mapping, Sequence
5	from typing import Any, TypeVar
6	
7	from app.services.errors import AppError
8	
9	
10	T = TypeVar("T")
11	
12	
13	def parse_pagination_args(
14	    args: Mapping[str, Any],
15	    default_page_size: int = 20,
16	    max_page_size: int = 100,
17	) -> dict[str, int | bool]:
18	    raw_page = args.get("page")
19	    raw_page_size = args.get("page_size")
20	
21	    if raw_page is None and raw_page_size is None:
22	        return {"requested": False, "page": 1, "page_size": default_page_size}
23	
24	    try:
25	        page = int(raw_page or "1")
26	        page_size = int(raw_page_size or str(default_page_size))
27	    except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
28	        raise AppError("validation_error", "page and page_size must be integers.", 400) from exc
29	
30	    if page < 1 or page_size < 1:
31	        raise AppError("validation_error", "page and page_size must be positive integers.", 400)
32	
33	    return {"requested": True, "page": page, "page_size": min(page_size, max_page_size)}
34	
35	
36	def paginate_collection(items: Sequence[T], pagination: dict[str, int | bool]) -> tuple[list[T], dict[str, int | bool]]:
37	    total_items = len(items)
38	    requested = bool(pagination["requested"])
39	
40	    if not requested:
41	        page = 1
42	        page_size = max(total_items, 1)
43	        page_items = list(items)
44	    else:
45	        page = int(pagination["page"])
46	        page_size = int(pagination["page_size"])
47	        start = (page - 1) * page_size
48	        end = start + page_size
49	        page_items = list(items[start:end])
50	
51	    if total_items == 0:
52	        total_pages = 1
53	    else:
54	        total_pages = max(1, math.ceil(total_items / page_size))
55	
56	    return page_items, {
57	        "page": page,
58	        "page_size": page_size,
59	        "total_items": total_items,
60	        "total_pages": total_pages,
61	        "has_next": page < total_pages,
62	        "has_prev": page > 1,
63	    }
64

23	    def get_or_create_cart(self, user_id: str) -> Cart:
24	        stmt = select(Cart).options(joinedload(Cart.items)).where(Cart.user_id == user_id, Cart.status == "active")
25	        cart = db.session.execute(stmt).unique().scalar_one_or_none()
26	        if cart is None:
27	            cart = Cart(user_id=user_id, status="active")
28	            db.session.add(cart)
29	            db.session.flush()
30	        return cart
31	
32	    def get_cart(self, user_id: str) -> Cart:
33	        stmt = (
34	            select(Cart)
35	            .options(joinedload(Cart.items))
36	            .where(Cart.user_id == user_id, Cart.status == "active")
37	        )
38	        cart = db.session.execute(stmt).unique().scalar_one_or_none()
39	        if cart is None:
40	            return self.get_or_create_cart(user_id)
41	        return cart
42	
43	    def get_cart_item(self, cart_id: str, item_id: str) -> CartItem | None:
44	        stmt = select(CartItem).where(CartItem.cart_id == cart_id, CartItem.id == item_id)
45	        return db.session.scalar(stmt)
46	
47	    def add_cart_item(self, **kwargs) -> CartItem:
48	        item = CartItem(**kwargs)
49	        db.session.add(item)
50	        db.session.flush()
51	        return item

53	    def delete_cart_item(self, item: CartItem) -> None:
54	        db.session.delete(item)
55	        db.session.flush()
56	
57	    def get_dish_for_update(self, dish_id: str) -> Dish | None:
58	        stmt = (
59	            select(Dish)
60	            .options(
61	                joinedload(Dish.options).joinedload(DishOption.values),
62	                joinedload(Dish.options).joinedload(DishOption.rules),
63	                joinedload(Dish.availability_windows),
64	            )
65	            .where(Dish.id == dish_id)
66	        )
67	        return db.session.execute(stmt).unique().scalar_one_or_none()
68	
69	    def find_order_by_checkout_key(self, user_id: str, checkout_key: str) -> Order | None:
70	        stmt = (
71	            select(Order)

73	            .where(Order.user_id == user_id, Order.checkout_key == checkout_key)
